concerns.text: FR-005 and US-3 require a 'corrected resampled t-test (Nadeau & Bengio)' on test set predictions. Task T025 describes implementing a 'Paired t-test' on CV scores without explicitly mentioning the 'corrected resampled' method or Nadeau & Bengio. This omission risks implementing a standard paired t-test (statistically invalid for CV) instead of the required method, leaving FR-005 unfulfilled.

SC-003 requires measuring if Morgan bits 'within radius 2' of the phosphorus atom exceed MACCS by ≥15%. Task T027 describes mapping bits 'within a defined radius' without explicitly enforcing 'radius=2'. This ambiguity allows implementation with a different radius, failing to satisfy the specific metric defined in SC-003.

The Edge Cases section in spec.md mandates that if the split fails (insufficient diversity), the system must 'output a report stating that statistical comparison is invalid'. Task T021 creates 'invalid_split_report.md', but Task T029 (final report generation) is marked incomplete. There is no explicit task ensuring the final 'research_results.md' (T029) is generated to reflect this 'invalid' status as required by the Edge Case logic, creating a gap in the output chain for this failure mode.

Task T001 defines the project directory as 'projects/PROJ-678-comparative-analysis-of-molecular-fingerprints/' but the actual artifact path and plan.md structure use 'projects/PROJ-678-comparative-analysis-of-molecular-finger/' (truncated). This inconsistency in the task description vs. the actual project path may cause implementation errors.

Task T031 references updating 'research.md' to document uncertainty exclusion. However, the plan.md and spec.md indicate 'research.md' is a Phase 0 output. While updating it is valid, the task description should explicitly link this to the 'Assumptions' section in spec.md to ensure the correct justification (Instrument Precision/Algorithm Calibration) is cited, rather than just 'Spec Assumptions' generally.
